message:
test(fcp-conformance/lease-coordinator): pin acquire/renew/release causality + fencing-token monotonicity (br-9nee8)

LeaseCoordinator is the per-subject lease authority across mesh nodes
— the routing-decision causality engine that decides which node may
execute singleton-writer operations. Zero conformance coverage today
even though it is the split-brain-defense backbone of the mesh.

Adds 10 tests against the public fcp_mesh::LeaseCoordinator API:

- acquire_on_empty_state_grants_lease_with_fresh_token
- acquire_when_other_holds_active_lease_returns_denied_with_holder_named
  (the routing-layer signal callers depend on to re-route, not fail)
- expired_lease_yields_grant_to_new_acquirer (time-domain causality:
  once now_secs >= expires_at, prior lease is no longer active)
- fencing_tokens_are_strictly_monotonic_across_consecutive_acquires
  (the deterministic split-brain resolver — every new lease MUST
  receive a token greater than any previously seen)
- renew_by_current_holder_extends_expiry
- renew_after_expiry_is_denied_with_no_active_lease (reason text
  surfaces 'no active lease' for triage)
- renew_with_wrong_fencing_token_is_denied
- release_by_current_holder_succeeds
- release_by_non_holder_returns_not_held
- release_with_wrong_token_returns_not_held

Mid-stream import fix: TailscaleNodeId is exposed via fcp_core, not
fcp_tailscale (the latter has TailscaleClient).

All 10 pass locally in 0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
lease_coordinator_causality_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
